title: Trace a scalar type-coercion bug
description: Find where a value was silently coerced by following it from input to the wrong-typed comparison or store.
body: Steps: 1. Identify the symptom (a numeric string compared loosely, a bool stored as 1, a null becoming 0). 2. Trace the value from its source (request, DB, JSON) to where it misbehaves. 3. Check whether the calling file declares strict_types and whether comparisons use == (loose) vs === (strict). 4. Add strict_types and a precise type hint at the boundary, switch to strict comparison. 5. Add a test passing a wrong-typed value to confirm it now errors instead of coercing. Rollback point: before tightening comparisons that other code may rely on. Validation: the boundary now rejects or correctly converts the value with a test proving it.
